evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` computes `RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_IMPORT_BUDGET` at module load via `new Date()`, making module state time-dependent at import time.
`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` similarly computes `LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET` at import time and keeps module-global mutable state (`hasWarnedPastRemovalTarget`).

`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` dynamically probes module exports (`getSupabaseClient` vs `supabase`) to bridge old/new runtime surfaces, which keeps boundary ambiguity alive.
cross_module_architecture.issues_preventing_higher_score: Legacy compatibility modules still carry time-based import-time state and dynamic fallback wiring, which increases coupling and makes boundaries less deterministic.

evidence: `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` (UI primitive layer) imports app-theme extension constants from `shared/components/theme/buttonVariants`, mixing foundation and app-specific concerns.

`src/shared/components/MediaTypeFilter.tsx` hardcodes concrete design-system variant names (`retro`, `zinc`, `retro-dark`) in feature-facing filter logic, exposing internal styling taxonomy beyond UI primitives.

Legacy compatibility surfaces remain first-class exports (`reportRuntimeError` in `handleError.ts`, `getLegacySupabaseClient` in `legacySupabaseFacade.ts`), so top-level ownership of 'current API' vs 'migration shim' is still blurred.

Task builders in `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` each define large, partially overlapping param bags and hand-map dozens of fields into payloads.

The same orchestration concepts (`orchestrator_task_id`, `run_id`, `parent_generation_id`, mode flags, lora/phase settings) are repeatedly translated with per-file fallback precedence, indicating abstraction leakage instead of a stable shared contract.

`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` exports multiple re-shaped state objects (`imageEditValue`, `inpaintingState`, `generationState`, `transformState`) that largely forward underlying hook values, increasing indirection cost.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` always writes `advanced_mode: true` while still exposing `motion_mode` that can be `'basic'`, creating contradictory handoff semantics between producer and consumers.
`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx` contains compensating interpretation logic (treat `motion_mode === 'basic'` as non-advanced) to recover from mixed signals in payloads.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/__tests__/joinClips.test.ts` explicitly locks in the always-advanced behavior, cementing seam inconsistency instead of resolving it.

Annotation UI logic is duplicated across `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx` (shape toggle + delete overlay controls), with different styling and local wiring.
Local implementation divergence is already visible (theme-token classes in InlineEditView vs hardcoded color classes in LightboxLayout), increasing drift risk for future behavior changes.
`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` contains a very large return assembly that manually mirrors many nested fields, making local edits error-prone and hard to verify.

`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` accepts `AbortSignal` but dedupes by `{toolId,projectId,shotId}` only, so later callers with different cancellation state are forced onto an existing promise (`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts:305-309`).

`composeTaskPayload` returns both top-level spread orchestrator fields and nested `orchestrator_details`, creating two competing representations of the same data (`src/shared/lib/tasks/taskPayloadContract.ts:52-55`), while readers then search across multiple payload locations (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelPayloadReader.ts:21-28`, `:30-46`).

`generateVideoService` documents `generateVideo()` as public API but exports many internal/test-only helpers plus backward-compat request shapes from the same module (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:580-603`, `:432-452`).

Multiple files are deprecation-only wrappers with no behavior (`src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.tsx`, `src/shared/components/AspectRatioSelector.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/useAsyncOperation.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useBottomOffset.ts`, `src/shared/lib/uiClassNames.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/runtime/clientAccess.ts`).
Comment density is high and often restates implementation details rather than non-obvious intent (`src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts`, `src/shared/lib/galleryUtils.ts`).
Pass-through helper with no transformation exists (`getAspectRatioStyle` in `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts` delegates directly to `getProjectAspectRatioStyle`).
ai_generated_debt.issues_preventing_higher_score: Generated-style ceremony (wrapper aliases + explanatory boilerplate) still adds noise and maintenance overhead across shared modules.

evidence: Legacy/new import paths are intentionally coexisting via deprecated alias files with the same removal target (`2026-06-30`) across shared hooks/components/libs and Supabase runtime access.
`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` still contains a full compatibility accessor path and phased import-budget constants, indicating migration debt remains active.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` keep dual schemas in active types and logic (`legacy_paths`, `video_edit_mode`, deprecated structure video fields).

The reviewed set includes 4 root-level compatibility alias files in high-traffic flat directories (`src/shared/hooks/useAsyncOperation.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts`, `src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.tsx`, `src/shared/components/AspectRatioSelector.tsx`) that only re-export canonical modules elsewhere.
`holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles["src/shared/hooks/"]` shows a flat directory with 88 production files; these alias stragglers add non-domain entries to an already overloaded package.

`holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles["src/shared/components/"]` shows 76 files with mixed responsibilities, while canonical ownership already moved to `src/app/components/error/` and `src/shared/components/generation-controls/`.

Lightbox flow uses oversized pass-through surfaces: `MediaLightboxProps` aggregates dozens of fields and re-bundles them (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx:11-77`, `:79-147`) before forwarding to specialized components.

`useSharedLightboxState` composes many concern-specific prop interfaces into one mega-input and returns a very broad state object spanning variants/navigation/star/references/lineage/shot/actions (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts:53-138`, `:144-240`).

Settings persistence logic is split between two mode-specific loaders and a large orchestrator hook, with duplicated state-machine handling across `useAutoSaveSettings` and `useDebouncedSettingsSave` (`src/shared/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:110-239`, `:296-590`; `src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts:145-250`).

Edge function request lifecycle conventions diverge within the same subsystem: some functions use `initEdgeRuntime` + `jsonResponse` + explicit OPTIONS handling (`supabase/functions/generate-pat/index.ts`, `supabase/functions/revoke-pat/index.ts`), while others hand-roll env/client/JSON/error wiring with raw `Response` (`supabase/functions/get-task-output/index.ts`, `supabase/functions/generate-upload-url/index.ts`).

Video frame extraction logic is duplicated across utilities (`src/shared/lib/videoPosterExtractor.ts:10-68`, `:75-133`; `src/shared/lib/videoThumbnailGenerator.ts:28-93`), indicating repeated low-level orchestration instead of a shared primitive.

`useTaskStatusCounts` still uses legacy accessor style (`getLegacySupabaseClient as supabase`, then `supabase()` per query) while sibling task hooks use `getSupabaseClient()` directly (`src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:2,51,59,71` vs `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:3,63` and `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:19,35,74`).

Legacy facade keeps compatibility fallback to `supabaseClientModule.supabase` (`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts:75-84`), so old/new call conventions are both still active in the same subsystem.

Sibling task-data hooks expose different failure contracts: `useTasks` throws on fetch failure via `handleAndRethrow` (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:42-49`), but `useTaskStatusCounts` swallows query errors, logs them, and returns a degraded success payload (`src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:49-134`).

Mutation flow in `createGeneration` can throw after creating the generation row (variant insert failure), leaving partial persisted state (`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:91-131`), which differs from the degraded-return pattern used in other task-facing hooks.
`calculate-task-cost` enforces explicit service-role authorization before privileged operations (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:288-299`).

Comparable privileged completion paths perform direct task updates without local auth/authorization assertions in their exported entry functions (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:315-323,569-577,634-636`; `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:180-187,331-334`), relying entirely on upstream caller discipline.

findings.summary: Legacy alias facades embed time-dependent state at import time
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports `RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_IMPORT_BUDGET = getRuntimeErrorAliasImportBudget()` where default argument uses `new Date()`.
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` exports `LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET = getLegacySupabaseImportBudget()` with the same date-at-import pattern.
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` keeps module-global mutable warning state (`hasWarnedPastRemovalTarget`), adding hidden process-lifetime behavior to a cross-module dependency.
findings.suggestion: Remove import-time budget constants and expose only pure functions that receive `now` explicitly (or inject a clock in policy checks). Move deprecation-budget tracking out of runtime facades into a dedicated diagnostics module so core imports are deterministic.

findings.summary: Supabase compatibility facade preserves dual runtime access paths
evidence: `getLegacySupabaseClient()` attempts `getSupabaseClient` first, then falls back to `supabase` export via dynamic property lookup and casting.
This keeps callers insulated from choosing one canonical runtime boundary, and the same pattern appears in error handling where deprecated alias export (`reportRuntimeError`) remains active.
Both files encode migration shims as primary import targets rather than isolating them to a narrow compatibility boundary.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a strict compatibility boundary package (for example `legacy/compat/*`) and route all deprecated aliases through that single location. Keep canonical runtime modules alias-free, then codemod callsites to canonical imports and fail CI on new imports from legacy paths.

findings.summary: Shared UI primitives mix foundational API with product theme specifics
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `APP_BUTTON_SIZES` and `APP_BUTTON_VARIANTS` from `shared/components/theme/buttonVariants`, so primitive contract depends on product-layer styling extensions.
`MediaTypeFilter.tsx` directly depends on internal style variant vocabulary (`retro`, `zinc`, `retro-dark`) instead of consuming a semantic intent API.
`CreativePartnerPane.tsx` uses raw `<button>` elements for CTA while other parts rely on shared primitives, reflecting unclear ownership of button interaction/styling boundaries at the top level.
findings.suggestion: Split UI into `ui/primitives` (no app theme imports) and `ui/patterns` (app-themed wrappers). Expose semantic variants (e.g., `surface='dark'`, `tone='muted'`) from wrappers, and migrate feature components to those wrappers so feature code does not depend on theme token names.

findings.summary: Media gallery item flow is fragmented into multiple bag-style contracts
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` accepts a wide argument bag containing navigation, modal controls, filter controls, and lightbox setters from different concerns.
`ShotActions` consumes four grouped prop objects (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) that are still tightly coupled and require synchronized field evolution.
`useMediaGalleryItemState` mixes independent concerns (star toggling, info tooltip state, drag state, shot modal flow) into one hook API, widening change surface for unrelated edits.
findings.suggestion: Define a single domain-level controller boundary for media-item actions (shot assignment, details/opening, navigation) and keep visual components dumb. Split state hooks by concern (`useShotAssignmentState`, `useItemOverlayState`) and pass one stable controller object to `ShotActions`.

findings.summary: Task creation uses duplicated wide param-bag mappers instead of one enforceable contract layer.
evidence: Each file builds large `Record<string, unknown>` payloads with repeated orchestration keys and per-file fallback order.
Similar concerns (family payload composition, routing metadata, lora/phase wiring) are re-implemented independently.
Resulting contracts are only partially typed at edges, so behavior depends on mapper-specific conditional spreads.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a schema-backed shared payload assembler for orchestration metadata and mode fields (task-family core + per-family extension). Move common precedence rules into that shared layer, and have each task module provide only minimal feature-specific deltas.

findings.summary: Join-clips payload sends conflicting mode signals across module boundaries.
evidence: `buildJoinClipsPayload` hardcodes `advanced_mode: true` while also emitting `motion_mode` from user settings.
Consumer logic in `VideoTravelDetails` must override/interpret this conflict (`motion_mode === 'basic'` treated as basic despite advanced flag).
Tests assert the hardcoded advanced flag, reinforcing contradictory seam behavior.
findings.suggestion: Make `advanced_mode` derived from a single canonical source (`motion_mode` or explicit advanced toggle) and remove consumer-side compensation. Update join-clips tests to assert coherent mode contracts rather than hardcoded advanced mode.

findings.summary: Annotation overlay controls are duplicated with diverging local implementations.
evidence: Both `InlineEditView` and `LightboxLayout` implement near-identical shape mode toggle/delete button overlays.
The duplicated implementations already diverge in class strategy and palette choices.
Stroke/selection behavior is centralized in `StrokeOverlay`, but control rendering is split, so behavior updates must be manually mirrored in two UI paths.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `AnnotationOverlayControls` component consumed by both layouts, with tokenized style variants (inline vs lightbox) and one prop contract for selected-shape actions.

findings.summary: Billing outcome persistence can be permanently skipped after orchestrator is already marked Complete.
evidence: `markOrchestratorComplete` updates orchestrator status to `Complete` first (`orchestrator.ts:620-637`) and only then triggers/persists billing outcome (`:655-666`).

If `persistBillingOutcome` throws (`orchestrator.ts:174-187`), outer flow throws recoverable error (`:473-485`), but subsequent invocations return early once status is `Complete` (`:372-374`), so billing reconciliation metadata may never be written.
findings.suggestion: Make completion + billing outcome idempotent as one lifecycle contract: either persist billing metadata before flipping to `Complete`, or allow post-complete retries to continue billing persistence when `billing_outcome`/`billing_reconciliation` is absent.

findings.summary: Single-flight dedupe in tool settings breaks per-caller abort semantics promised by the API.
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` takes `signal?: AbortSignal` (`toolSettingsService.ts:291-295`) but cached in-flight promise is keyed only by scope identifiers (`:305-309`).

A caller can pass an already-aborted or later-aborted signal yet still receive a non-cancelled shared promise from another caller, so cancellation behavior is inconsistent for the same function signature.
findings.suggestion: Define one contract and enforce it: either remove per-call abort support when deduping globally, or include caller-scoped cancellation mediation (e.g., return a wrapper promise that races shared fetch with local abort and maps to `cancelled`).

findings.summary: Task payload composition duplicates orchestrator data across top-level and nested fields, creating ambiguous read behavior.
evidence: `composeTaskPayload` returns `{ ...orchestratorDetails, ...buildTaskPayloadContract(...) }` (`taskPayloadContract.ts:52-55`), so identical semantic fields can exist in two locations.

`travelPayloadReader` then resolves values by precedence across multiple sources (`travelPayloadReader.ts:21-28`, `:51-63`), meaning source-of-truth depends on fallback order rather than a single canonical schema.
findings.suggestion: Choose a canonical payload boundary (prefer nested contract blocks) and stop spreading orchestrator details into top-level transport objects; provide explicit migration adapters for legacy readers until removed.

findings.summary: Core orchestration services mix canonical and legacy compatibility paths in the same modules, increasing seam entropy.
evidence: `generateVideoService` includes both v2 request model and backward-compat input fields in one builder path (`generateVideoService.ts:420-452`, `:454-577`).
`joinClipsCompatAdapter` maps many legacy fields into canonical format while still exposing the old broad params surface (`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts:19-83`, `:173-245`).
findings.suggestion: Split compatibility adapters from canonical orchestration services into separate modules and keep runtime service APIs canonical-only; confine legacy mapping to thin ingress boundaries with explicit deprecation telemetry.

findings.summary: Deprecated alias modules keep old import surface alive across core shared paths.
evidence: Each file is a pure re-export compatibility shim with `@deprecated` and `@removalTarget 2026-06-30`.
No local behavior exists in these files, so they only preserve legacy import paths and increase migration duration.
findings.suggestion: Run a codemod to rewrite imports to canonical targets, then enforce a lint rule (`no-restricted-imports`) on these legacy paths and delete alias files in one tracked removal PR.

findings.summary: Join-clips/travel payloads still support legacy and canonical fields in one API.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` includes both canonical (`clip_source`, `video_edit`) and legacy (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`) fields.
Adapter logic must validate and infer mode boundaries (`validateCompatModeBoundaries`, `resolveClipSource`, `resolveVideoEditConfig`).
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` still exports deprecated field contracts for structure-video migration, and `galleryUtils` explicitly handles multiple historical param shapes.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy parsing into a dedicated boundary translator module, make core task creators accept canonical-only DTOs, and gate legacy translator usage behind explicit telemetry until callers are migrated.

findings.summary: Several files contain generated-style ceremony with low signal-to-noise.
evidence: `image-utils.ts` includes long explanatory blocks and a pass-through helper (`getAspectRatioStyle`) that adds no domain logic.
`galleryUtils.ts` has verbose narration around straightforward extraction flow.
Multiple one-line deprecated re-export files are wrapped in full header blocks, repeating migration metadata with no local behavior.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to decision-relevant context only, remove zero-value pass-through helpers, and consolidate repeated deprecation metadata into a migration tracker doc plus lint-enforced ban list.

findings.summary: Legacy accessors use branch-heavy defensive probing for simple runtime reads.
evidence: `getLegacySupabaseClient` performs two `try/catch` blocks around property access and multiple `unknown` casts before fallback/throw.
`clientAccess.ts` and `handleError.ts` show similar migration-era alias maintenance, increasing local control-flow and symbol overhead around otherwise simple APIs.
findings.suggestion: Replace runtime probing with a typed compatibility adapter that imports explicit symbols once, fail fast with one clear branch, and isolate alias constants/metadata in a separate migration manifest.

findings.summary: Deprecated compatibility entrypoints remain as root-package stragglers and obscure canonical folder ownership.
evidence: All four files are pure re-export shims with deprecation metadata and `@removalTarget 2026-06-30`, but still occupy primary root package locations.
Canonical modules already exist in focused target packages (`shared/hooks/async`, `shared/hooks/media`, `app/components/error`, `shared/components/generation-controls`).

Directory profiles show these roots are already large (`src/shared/hooks/` 88 files, `src/shared/components/` 76 files), so keeping one-line migration files there increases navigational noise and false package ownership signals.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Create dedicated migration alias folders (`src/shared/hooks/legacy/aliases/`, `src/shared/components/legacy/aliases/`) and move these four shims there first. 2) Update imports with `rg "@/shared/(hooks/useAsyncOperation|hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator|components/AppErrorBoundary|components/AspectRatioSelector)" -n src` then replace to canonical paths (or temporary new alias paths) in descending fan-in order. 3) Keep temporary re-exports only in legacy folders with one index barrel per legacy folder. 4) Validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and targeted tests `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAsyncOperation.contract.test.ts src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx`. 5) Remove legacy alias folders at deprecation date after `rg` confirms zero imports.

findings.summary: The shared layer still acts as a compatibility gateway across domains, weakening top-level architectural clarity.
evidence: `AppErrorBoundary` now belongs to `app/components/error` but remains exposed through `shared/components`, creating a misleading top-level contract.
`AspectRatioSelector` ownership migrated to `shared/components/generation-controls` while legacy root export remains the visible entrypoint.
Contract tests currently verify legacy and canonical exports are identical, which preserves behavior but also institutionalizes legacy entrypoints in the primary package structure.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Introduce explicit ownership entry barrels in canonical domains (`src/app/components/error/index.ts`, `src/shared/components/generation-controls/index.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/async/index.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/media/index.ts`). 2) Move migration-contract tests to dedicated folders (`src/shared/components/legacy/__tests__/`, `src/shared/hooks/legacy/__tests__/`) so architectural tests no longer live beside active domain tests. 3) Update import callsites to canonical owners using `rg "@/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary|@/shared/components/AspectRatioSelector|@/shared/hooks/useAsyncOperation|@/shared/hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator" -n src` followed by codemod/manual replacement. 4) Validate boundaries with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts`, and a final `rg` check showing only legacy-folder references remain.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade keeps deprecated and canonical runtime contracts coupled.
evidence: `getLegacySupabaseClient()` reflectively falls back to both `getSupabaseClient` and legacy `supabase`, so the old API remains a live runtime branch.

README states canonical runtime boot through `initializeSupabase()`/`getSupabaseClient()` with legacy path as compatibility-only, but facade logic preserves dual-path behavior indefinitely unless manually removed.
Coupling matrix reports strong dependency into legacy path (`src/shared/hooks/ → src/integrations/supabase/legacy/ => 36`).
findings.suggestion: Replace reflective dual-access with a single delegated call to `getSupabaseClient()` and fail-fast when bootstrap was not initialized; keep migration pressure at compile/import level (budget checks) instead of runtime fallback behavior. Then remove exported legacy `supabase` fallback branch and validate with `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage` and `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Runtime modules embed governance policy constants instead of consuming a single contract source.
evidence: Both files define owner/removal/import-budget constants directly next to runtime behavior (`handleError`, `getLegacySupabaseClient`).
README independently repeats governance targets/gates, creating multiple truth sources for the same contract lifecycle data.
This structure forces policy edits to touch docs plus multiple runtime files, increasing drift and review surface.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a centralized contract manifest (for example `src/contracts/deprecations/*`) for owner/removal/budget metadata, import it from runtime shims, and generate/sync README contract snippets from that manifest. Keep runtime files focused on behavior and contract files focused on governance metadata.

findings.summary: Temporary compatibility and mixed-concern UI utilities are organized as core stable packages.
evidence: Directory profile shows `src/shared/components/ui/` has 58 files and mixed roles, exceeding flat-folder cohesion thresholds.
Legacy Supabase shim lives under `src/integrations/supabase/` despite being explicitly temporary in README policy, and has high live usage via hooks coupling.
Canonical contracts for compatibility are documented in README but not reflected by dedicated package boundaries that distinguish stable runtime vs transitional compatibility.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Create `src/contracts/deprecations/` (contract metadata) and `src/compat/supabase/` (temporary adapters). 2) Move `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` into `src/compat/supabase/` and keep `src/integrations/supabase/` canonical-only. 3) Split `src/shared/components/ui/` into `ui/primitives/` (button, cn, Slot, controls) and `ui/runtime/` (presenter/registration wiring). 4) Update aliases/imports with `rg -n "integrations/supabase/legacy|shared/components/ui/" src README.md` and project-wide replace. 5) Validate with `npm run check:contracts`, `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage`, `npm run check:error-runtime-alias-usage`, and `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Lightbox architecture relies on large pass-through prop bags and orchestration wrappers.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` is a broad mixed interface (`MediaLightbox.tsx:11-77`) and `useBundledLightboxProps` mostly repackages fields without behavior (`:79-147`).
`ImageLightboxProps` repeats grouped-plus-local concerns and feeds multiple orchestration hooks (`ImageLightbox.tsx:66-86`, `:88-190`).

`UseSharedLightboxStateProps` merges six concern interfaces plus external callbacks (`useSharedLightboxState.ts:127-138`) and returns a very wide object (`:144-240`) that mixes unrelated state domains.
findings.suggestion: Split lightbox into explicit boundary contracts per concern (navigation, shot-actions, edit-mode, metadata) and move bundling to typed adapters near call-sites. Reduce each adapter to one responsibility and keep `useSharedLightboxState` focused on truly shared media-agnostic state only.

findings.summary: A single settings hook carries two persistence models, creating mixed responsibilities.
evidence: `useAutoSaveSettings` contains custom-mode loading, React Query-mode loading, entity-transition state resets, pending-save transitions, and full CRUD helpers in one hook (`useAutoSaveSettings.ts:296-662`).
Mode-specific load flows duplicate lifecycle mechanics (`useCustomModeLoad` vs `useReactQueryModeLoad`, `useAutoSaveSettings.ts:110-239`) and then rely on shared refs/state machine synchronization.
`useDebouncedSettingsSave` duplicates mode branching again for flush/save paths (`useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts:183-217`, `:219-260`), increasing cross-hook coupling and change risk.
findings.suggestion: Split into mode-specific public hooks (e.g., `useAutoSaveSettingsRQ` and `useAutoSaveSettingsCustom`) that share a minimal persistence-agnostic core. Keep mode branching out of runtime hot paths and remove duplicated lifecycle coordination.

findings.summary: Edge functions implement incompatible request/runtime patterns inside one API surface.
evidence: `generate-pat` and `revoke-pat` use shared runtime bootstrap (`initEdgeRuntime`) and shared response helper (`jsonResponse`) with explicit OPTIONS handling.

`get-task-output` and `generate-upload-url` reimplement env lookup, client creation, JSON parsing, and raw `Response` formatting (`get-task-output/index.ts:31-63`, `:129-143`; `generate-upload-url/index.ts:37-65`, `:182-191`).
This inconsistency forces per-function error/CORS/auth behavior decisions rather than one subsystem contract.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a single edge-handler template (runtime init, auth gate, body parsing, response/error helpers, CORS behavior) and migrate outlier functions to it, keeping endpoint-specific logic isolated from transport scaffolding.

findings.summary: Video frame extraction is duplicated across utilities instead of a shared primitive.
evidence: `extractVideoPosterFrame`/`extractVideoFinalFrame` and `extractThumbnailFromVideo` each reimplement video element setup, metadata/seek handling, canvas draw, blob conversion, and error listeners.
This creates structurally parallel logic with slight drift (e.g., different cleanup/error behavior), making reliability fixes require multi-file edits.
findings.suggestion: Create a single low-level `extractVideoFrame({source,time,scale,crossOrigin})` utility and make poster/thumbnail helpers thin configuration wrappers. Centralize cleanup/listener removal so failure handling is uniform.

findings.summary: Task hooks mix legacy and modern Supabase client conventions in the same flow family.
evidence: `useTaskStatusCounts` imports `getLegacySupabaseClient as supabase` and calls `supabase()` repeatedly (`src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:2,51,59,71`).
`useTasks` and `useGenerationMutations` use `getSupabaseClient()` directly (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:3,63`; `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:19,35,74`).
Legacy facade still supports fallback to `supabaseClientModule.supabase` (`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts:75-84`), preserving two patterns instead of one.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all task/generation hooks on `getSupabaseClient()` and remove legacy accessor usage from `useTaskStatusCounts`; then tighten `legacySupabaseFacade` to throw unless explicitly opted-in by a migration shim to prevent convention drift.

findings.summary: Task-related hooks use conflicting failure contracts (throw vs degraded success vs partial write).
evidence: `useTasks` propagates failures by throwing in query functions (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:42-49`).
`useTaskStatusCounts` catches per-query failures, logs, and returns `degraded: true` instead of failing the query (`src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:80-134`).
`createGeneration` can create a generation row and then throw on variant creation (`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:91-131`), producing an error after a side effect has already committed.
findings.suggestion: Define a single task-domain error policy (for example: always reject queries, use explicit `OperationResult` only for opt-in degraded endpoints) and apply it across task hooks; for `createGeneration`, add transactional/compensating behavior (rollback inserted generation or atomically create both records server-side).

findings.summary: Privileged completion modules rely on implicit caller trust instead of explicit auth assertions.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` validates both authentication success and service-role authorization before acting (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:288-299`).

`checkOrchestratorCompletion` and downstream helpers perform task state updates with no equivalent assertion in this module (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:315-323,569-577,634-636`).
`createGenerationFromTask` writes task flags without local authorization guard (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:180-187,331-334`).
findings.suggestion: Add a shared authorization invariant helper for complete-task exported entrypoints (assert privileged execution context before any update) and call it at the start of orchestration/generation completion flows to match enforcement rigor used by service-role endpoints.

findings.summary: Supabase runtime access still spans canonical, compatibility, and legacy entry paths, weakening boundary integrity.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` catches uninitialized runtime and falls back to lazy initialization (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:20-47`).
Legacy facade uses reflective access + compatibility fallback (`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts:62-84`).
Deprecated runtime compatibility re-export remains (`src/integrations/supabase/runtime/clientAccess.ts:1-7`).
findings.suggestion: Converge on a single runtime accessor contract: (1) freeze new imports to canonical client path, (2) replace facade callers with codemod + import lint rule, (3) remove `runtime/clientAccess.ts` re-export, (4) turn lazy fallback into hard fail after migration window, validated with `rg "legacySupabaseFacade|runtime/clientAccess" src` and full test run.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase migration is still operationally active instead of isolated to a shrinking compatibility edge.
evidence: Facade includes phased import budgets and active removal target metadata (`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts:7-14`, `50-60`).
Canonical client still preserves fallback init behavior specifically for legacy callers (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:20-47`).
Deprecated compatibility re-export remains present (`src/integrations/supabase/runtime/clientAccess.ts:1-7`).
findings.suggestion: Execute migration in dated stages: Stage 1 (now) block new legacy imports and inventory current users; Stage 2 move remaining callers to `getSupabaseClient()` plus explicit bootstrap ordering; Stage 3 remove facade compatibility branch and deprecated re-export; Stage 4 enforce via CI check (`rg "legacySupabaseFacade|runtime/clientAccess" src` must be empty) and run `pnpm test`/`pnpm vitest`.

findings.summary: Adjacent service hooks expose mixed error contracts (throw, wrapped throw, and fallback returns), making failure handling unpredictable.
evidence: `useApiTokens` catches and `handleAndRethrow`s on fetch/mutations (`src/features/account/hooks/useApiTokens.ts:41-46`, `59-64`, `77-82`).
`useCredits` throws new generic errors from data fetchers (`src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits.ts:62-99`) and separately catches/rethrows in mutation paths (`160-165`, `211-216`).

`useAIInteractionService` often catches and returns fallback values (`null`, `[]`, `{success:false}`) unless optional throw mode is enabled (`src/features/ai/hooks/useAIInteractionService.ts:48-57`, `103-112`, `138-147`).
findings.suggestion: Standardize hook-level failure contract with one pattern (recommended: typed `Result` for user-interaction hooks, throw for low-level fetchers), then refactor in order: `useAIInteractionService` -> `useCredits` -> `useApiTokens`/`useApiKeys`; add contract tests asserting error behavior per public method.

findings.summary: Gallery page state abstraction is overloaded with multiple concerns and compatibility aliasing, reducing clarity and leverage.
evidence: `useGalleryPageState` combines filter state, pagination resets, query fallback, multiple mutations, event dispatch, and toast UX in one unit (`src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts:57-311`).
Legacy naming bridge (`useGalleryPageState = useGenerationsPageLogic`) keeps duplicate conceptual API surface (`src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts:323`).
findings.suggestion: Split into focused hooks in staged order: (1) `useGalleryFiltersAndPaging`, (2) `useGalleryMutations`, (3) `useGalleryFallbackBehavior`; then keep one exported façade and remove legacy alias after caller migration; validate with hook tests and `rg "useGenerationsPageLogic" src` cleanup.

findings.summary: Realtime instrumentation relies on global monkey-patching without explicit teardown contracts, reducing runtime/test composability.
evidence: `console.warn` is patched in-place in realtime instrumentation (`src/integrations/supabase/instrumentation/realtime/index.ts:111-137`).
`window.onerror`, `window.onunhandledrejection`, and `window.WebSocket` are overwritten in window instrumentation (`src/integrations/supabase/instrumentation/window/index.ts:39-144`).
Initializer installs instrumentation but exposes no uninstall lifecycle (`src/integrations/supabase/debug/initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals.ts:21-29`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce install/uninstall handles for each instrumentation module and register them in a runtime lifecycle manager; stage rollout: (1) return disposer closures from install functions, (2) wire teardown in dev hot-reload/test setup, (3) add idempotence tests ensuring original globals are restored.
